perturbation.original_text: Inria may terminate this Agreement, or suspend the delivery of data hereunder, for cause at any time if Organization breaches this Agreement or without cause at an y time for whatever reason.
perturbation.changed_text: Inria may terminate this Agreement, or suspend the delivery of data hereunder, for cause at any time if Organization breaches this Agreement, but only with 90 days prior written notice, or without cause only within the first 30 days of the agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding Inria's ability to terminate the agreement. The original text states that Inria may terminate the agreement with or without cause at any time. The modified text states that Inria can only terminate for cause with 90 days written notice, or without cause only within the first 30 days of the agreement. This creates an in-text contradiction making the termination clause, as it restricts Inria's rights compared to what was previously stated, potentially leading to confusion or disputes about termination rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: C. Duration . This Agreement will commence on the Effective Date and continue for 365 days thereafter (the “Agreement Term” ), at which point it shall automatically renew for an additional 365 days unless either party provides written notice of termination at least 30 days prior to the expiration date. Inria may terminate this Agreement, or suspend the delivery of data hereunder, for cause at any time if Organization breaches this Agreement, but only with 90 days prior written notice, or without cause only within the first 30 days of the agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states Inria can terminate the agreement at any time with or without cause. The changed text modifies this, stating that Inria can only terminate for cause with 90 days' notice or without cause only within the first 30 days of the agreement. The contradicted text section includes the changed text within it, which inherently creates an in-text contradiction since the initial statement of 'at any time' conflicts directly with the later restrictions placed on the termination clause. This makes Inria's termination rights unclear and creates a direct conflict based on the perturbation explanation and falls under the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The GTL Data remain the exclusive so le property of Inria. The present Agreement does not grant any transfer or assignment of commercial/exploitation/property rights on identified know -how, patents, software or any other intellectual property right held by Inria to the Organization.
perturbation.changed_text: The GTL Data remain the exclusive sole property of Inria. Upon written request, Inria grants the Organization a non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use the GTL Data for commercial research purposes, subject to Inria's prior written approval of each specific use case. The present Agreement does not grant any transfer or assignment of commercial/exploitation/property rights on identified know -how, patents, software or any other intellectual property right held by Inria to the Organization.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the Organization's rights to use the GTL Data. The original text states the agreement does not grant any commercial or exploitation rights. The modified text grants the Organization a non-exclusive license for commercial research, contradicting the previous statement. Now it has created an in-text contradiction, thus creating uncertainty about the intended use of the GTL data and the degree of control Inria retains, potentially leading to disagreements or legal challenges.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.GTL DATA. The GTL Data is comprised of the GTL traffic data and information that may be provided hereunder to Organization by Inria from time to time through CSV or Database format in the NECS Team Developers Zone . The GTL Data remain the exclusive sole property of Inria. Upon written request, Inria grants the Organization a non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use the GTL Data for commercial research purposes, subject to Inria's prior written approval of each specific use case. The present Agreement does not grant any transfer or assignment of commercial/exploitation/property rights on identified know -how, patents, software or any other intellectual property right held by Inria to the Organization. Promptly upon expiration or termination of this Agreement, user will cease using all GTL Data, and will return to Inria, or destroy, all originals and all copies of the GTL Data and other materials provided by Inria under this Agreement, and also certify such return or destruction in a reasonable and customary certificate provided by Inria. The obligation of confidentiality will remain until the GTL data are released to public or have fallen into public domain.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that the agreement does not grant any commercial or exploitation rights. The changed text introduces a non-exclusive license for commercial research purposes, which directly contradicts the previous statement denying any transfer or assignment of commercial/exploitation rights. The contradicted text is the entire modified section which creates the contradiction. This creates a strong in-text contradiction as it introduces uncertainty about the Organization's rights to use the GTL data for commercial purposes when it was originally forbidden. This aligns with the definition of inconsistencies, thus leading to a 'YES'.

perturbation.original_text: Promptly upon expiration or termination of this Agreement, user will cease using all GTL Data, and will return to Inria, or destroy, all originals and all copies of the GTL Data and other materials provided by Inria under this Agreement, and also certify such return or destruction in a reasonable and customary certificate provided by Inria.
perturbation.changed_text: Promptly upon expiration or termination of this Agreement, user will cease using all GTL Data, and will return to Inria, or destroy, all originals and all copies of the GTL Data and other materials provided by Inria under this Agreement, unless Inria grants a written extension for continued use. User will also certify such return or destruction in a reasonable and customary certificate provided by Inria.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by adding an exception to the requirement to return or destroy the GTL Data. The original text states that the user will cease using and return/destroy the data upon expiration/termination. The modified text adds that this doesn't apply if Inria grants a written extension for continued use. This in-text contradiction will create conflict regarding the right to continue to hold the GTL data.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.GTL DATA. The GTL Data is comprised of the GTL traffic data and information that may be provided hereunder to Organization by Inria from time to time through CSV or Database format in the NECS Team Developers Zone . The GTL Data remain the exclusive sole property of Inria. Upon written request, Inria grants the Organization a non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use the GTL Data for commercial research purposes, subject to Inria's prior written approval of each specific use case. The present Agreement does not grant any transfer or assignment of commercial/exploitation/property rights on identified know -how, patents, software or any other intellectual property right held by Inria to the Organization. Promptly upon expiration or termination of this Agreement, user will cease using all GTL Data, and will return to Inria, or destroy, all originals and all copies of the GTL Data and other materials provided by Inria under this Agreement, unless Inria grants a written extension for continued use. User will also certify such return or destruction in a reasonable and customary certificate provided by Inria. The obligation of confidentiality will remain until the GTL data are released to public or have fallen into public domain.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates the user to cease using, return, or destroy all GTL Data upon expiration or termination of the agreement. The changed text introduces an exception to this requirement, allowing continued use if Inria grants a written extension. The contradicted text contains the same language as the changed text, so the contradiction is within that single section of text. This creates a direct conflict because the original obligation to cease use and return/destroy the data is now conditional, undermining the initial clarity and creating a contradiction.
